seoTitle: How to Add Drill Through in Power BI Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to configure a cross-page drillthrough action in Power BI so executive summaries link directly to itemized detail pages.
intro: Breaking down an executive summary into itemized transaction rows works best as a separate, dedicated page rather than cramming everything onto one crowded canvas. This guide walks through setting up a drillthrough page, mapping the shared field that connects it to your summary visuals, and testing the navigation flow using the right-click context menu. It's aimed at report developers building investigative, click-to-explore dashboards.

steps.heading: Build your detail page
steps.body: Navigate to the sub-page intended for granular analysis and confirm its Page type is set to Standard under Page information.

steps.heading: Locate the drill-through field well
steps.body: Scroll down in the Visualizations pane below the standard formatting properties to find the specialized Drill-through data well.

steps.heading: Map your shared field
steps.body: Drag your primary categorical field from the Data pane and drop it into the Drill-through fields container.

steps.heading: Check the automatic back button
steps.body: Confirm that Power BI has placed a functional Back button in the top-left of your detailed canvas.

steps.heading: Test the drillthrough action
steps.body: On your main summary page, right-click a data category or bar, hover over Drill-through, and select your target page to confirm filtering works.

faq.question: What field should I use to connect a drillthrough action?
faq.answer: Use a shared categorical field that exists on both your summary and detail pages, such as a product or region category.

faq.question: Does the back button need to be built manually?
faq.answer: No, Power BI automatically injects a styled Back button on your detail page once a drillthrough field is configured.

faq.question: How do I trigger a drillthrough as a viewer?
faq.answer: Right-click a data point on the summary page, hover over Drill-through, and select the target detail page from the context menu.

faq.question: Can I test drillthrough without leaving Desktop?
faq.answer: Yes, you can hold Ctrl and click your Back button or use the right-click context menu directly inside Power BI Desktop to test the flow.
